page: Amazon Web Services c6g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by the custom 64-core AWS Graviton2 processor using the arm64 architecture. It features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation, running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators included. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory bandwidth efficiency. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU stress tests, software compilation with Clang, and high-throughput Redis SET operations. Static web serving throughput is also highly competitive, reaching 46.72 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more moderate, as evidenced by average stress-ng single-core scores and weaker single-threaded compression and decompression metrics. Large-block uncached memory read and write speeds fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture eliminates hypervisor overhead, making it highly efficient for workloads that benefit from direct hardware access and high physical core density. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio is optimized for compute-heavy tasks rather than memory-intensive databases. Tradeoffs include the lack of local NVMe storage and lower single-threaded performance compared to x86 alternatives. This server is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation pipelines, high-concurrency web serving, and Redis caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 AWS Graviton2 processor. Operating at 2.5 GHz, it is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 25 Gbps baseline network interface, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core performance, particularly in software compilation, Redis operations, and static web serving, alongside average single-core and large-block memory bandwidth metrics. The lack of hypervisor overhead provides high resource density and efficiency for multi-threaded workloads. This server is highly suited for high-concurrency web serving, CI/CD compilation pipelines, and distributed caching systems.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 physical arm64 cores, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 arm64 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network speed, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, 64-core ARM-based server delivering high multi-threaded efficiency and dedicated compute resources for high-concurrency workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical arm64 cores running at 2.5 GHz
128.0 GB of DDR4 memory with 2.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and Redis operations
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and CI/CD pipelines
